article.title: Uber LLD Round
article.content:
What is expectation in Uber LLD Round?

Is there some skeleton/boiler plate code and we have to extend on it and just complete few methods OR are we expected to write everything from scratch?

Is there discussion on classes/objects?

post.content: your code should run at the end thats non negotiable! all other things can be compromised upto an extent

uber lld rounds usually expect you to build things from scratch tbh, not just fill in a skeleton. sometimes they might give a basic problem statement or constraints, but you\u2019re generally responsible for designing the classes, objects, and interactions yourself. there\u2019s definitely discussion around class design, responsibilities, relationships, and how data flows, more than just writing methods. they care about how you structure things, handle edge cases, and explain tradeoffs. it\u2019s less about perfect code and more about clear design thinking and communication.
`Is there discussion on classes/objects?`\n\nDepends on the tech stack you are being interviewed for and the role, if you are going to work on JAVA most probably yes...
Try to share interview experience till now when you get time
